_comment: Polygon bevel on an IRREGULAR quad (cube top face stretched along x: v_2 and v_6 moved → top face becomes a 1.6×1 trapezoid). Tests perpendicular-edge-offset semantics on a non-square face — every boundary edge moves inward by `insert` distance regardless of edge length. With a multiplicative-scale formula the long edges would move 1.6× as much as the short edges, producing different geometry. The fact that this case PASSES bit-for-bit against Blender (which uses the same offsetMeet formula) confirms the MODO Inset semantics.
